spec_match.rationale: The page now wires a named view timeline through `timeline-scope: --article-progress` and demonstrates `animation-range: scroll 0% scroll 100%` with parser, computed, and current-time readouts.

interactive.rationale: The range lab provides a scroll slider, top/middle/bottom buttons, range branch buttons, and live offset/support/computed readouts.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The feature index promises a full-scroll reading progress bar, and the page now exposes the named view timeline contract plus the fallback when this browser cannot drive it.

edge_cases.rationale: The page covers unsupported or stuck CSS timeline progress and a malformed `scroll 0% scroll not-a-length` declaration without pretending the CSS branch passed.

references.rationale: The page includes the required ChromeStatus link for feature 6522328437620736.

design_system.rationale: The touched UI uses shared CSS variables, paired button colors, and no source inline handlers, source inline styles, or raw hex colors.

summary: Verified with chrome-devtools-mcp at `/v147/timeline-named-range-scroll/progress-tracker/?verify=after-timeline-final`: bottom scrolling reaches a full-width measured fallback when the named view timeline current time stays inactive, the invalid range is rejected, the ChromeStatus link is present, and the console has no warnings or errors.
